content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Debugging Struggles
content.seo_description: Master SaaS development as a student. Overcome debugging roadblocks using a robust starter kit, Antigravity, and Claude Code. Build your portfolio today.
content.answer_block: Debugging is the primary bottleneck for student developers. By using a standardized SaaS starter kit, you enforce architectural consistency that minimizes runtime errors. When you do encounter bugs, leverage AI-driven tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to perform root-cause analysis, transforming complex errors into clear, actionable refactoring tasks instantly.
content.intro:
The screen glows with a sea of red error logs, and your momentum grinds to a halt. You’ve spent hours chasing a recursive loop or a silent authentication failure that makes no sense. The frustration of being unable to debug isn't a sign of failure; it's the rite of passage for every senior engineer who once stood exactly where you are today.

But what if you didn't have to fight the infrastructure alone? A pre-built SaaS starter kit acts as your architectural safety net. By combining this foundation with the precise diagnostic power of Antigravity and Claude Code, you shift from 'guessing' to 'engineering.' It is time to stop wrestling with your environment and start building the features that actually define your career.

steps.title: Initialize the Modular Architecture
steps.description: Standardize your project structure to ensure debugging is predictable. A clean starter kit separates concerns, making it easier to isolate failures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this directory structure and suggest a modular refactor for a SaaS starter kit that separates API routes, database models, and UI components for easier debugging.

steps.title: Automate Error Boundary Implementation
steps.description: Implement global error boundaries to catch crashes before they break the user experience. This turns silent failures into actionable logs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust React ErrorBoundary component that captures stack traces and logs them to a mock service for easier debugging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add error-boundary to main layout'

steps.title: Inject Diagnostic Middleware
steps.description: Add request-response logging to your API layer so you can trace the lifecycle of every data packet through your system.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Node.js/Express middleware that logs incoming request headers, body, and outgoing status codes for debugging purposes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement request logger in server middleware'

steps.title: Isolate Logic with Unit Tests
steps.description: Don't debug in the browser. Use unit tests to verify your business logic in isolation, preventing the snowball effect of compounding errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test suite for the authentication service using Jest, focusing on edge cases like expired tokens and malformed payloads.

steps.title: Perform AI-Assisted Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: When a bug persists, use Claude Code to parse the entire codebase context to find the exact line causing the conflict.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this failing test result and the associated code file; provide a step-by-step fix for the underlying logic error.

pro_tips: Always commit to a new branch before running AI-suggested refactors so you can revert instantly if the fix introduces regressions.
Use 'Type-Driven Development' by defining strict interfaces first; this eliminates 80% of runtime errors before they occur.
Configure your IDE to show hidden files to ensure you aren't missing environment configuration bugs.
When debugging, ask the AI to 'explain the logic flow' rather than just 'fix the code' to improve your own mental model.
Keep a 'Bug Journal' in a markdown file within your repo to track recurring patterns and avoid repeating the same mistake.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is stabilized, begin integrating a real database and deploy to Vercel or Railway to test your error handling in a production environment.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework and the tools to conquer any bug that stands in the way of your vision. Stop debugging in the dark and start building with the precision of a lead engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
